metadata.description: Singapore H2 Economics (9570) curriculum with full subtopic coverage and prerequisite ordering

nodes.label: Scarcity & Choice
nodes.description: The fundamental economic problem: unlimited wants vs limited resources; the need to make choices

nodes.label: Opportunity Cost
nodes.description: The next best alternative forgone when a choice is made; applies to individuals, firms and governments

nodes.label: Production Possibility Curve
nodes.description: PPC and its properties; constant vs increasing opportunity cost; shifts of the PPC; productive and allocative efficiency

nodes.label: Resource Allocation Mechanisms
nodes.description: Market mechanism, planned economy, and mixed economy approaches to answering what, how, and for whom to produce

nodes.label: Demand
nodes.description: Law of demand; individual and market demand; movement along vs shifts of the demand curve; determinants of demand

nodes.label: Supply
nodes.description: Law of supply; individual and market supply; movement along vs shifts of the supply curve; determinants of supply

nodes.label: Market Equilibrium
nodes.description: Equilibrium price and quantity; effects of shifts in demand and supply; simultaneous shifts; price adjustment mechanism

nodes.label: Consumer & Producer Surplus
nodes.description: Consumer surplus and producer surplus; total social surplus; deadweight loss; welfare analysis of markets

nodes.label: Price Elasticity of Demand
nodes.description: Definition, formula and calculation of PED; determinants; elastic, inelastic and unit elastic demand; relationship with total revenue

nodes.label: Price Elasticity of Supply
nodes.description: Definition, formula and calculation of PES; determinants; factors affecting time horizon and production flexibility

nodes.label: Income Elasticity of Demand
nodes.description: Definition, formula and calculation of YED; normal goods vs inferior goods; necessities vs luxuries; applications

nodes.label: Cross Elasticity of Demand
nodes.description: Definition, formula and calculation of XED; substitutes (positive XED) vs complements (negative XED); applications to firm strategy

nodes.label: Applications of Elasticity
nodes.description: Using elasticity concepts for government policy (tax incidence), business pricing decisions, and revenue analysis

nodes.label: Market Failure: Overview
nodes.description: Definition of market failure; allocative inefficiency; divergence between private and social costs/benefits; reasons why free markets may fail

nodes.label: Externalities
nodes.description: Positive and negative externalities in consumption and production; MPC, MSC, MPB, MSB analysis; welfare loss diagrams

nodes.label: Public Goods
nodes.description: Non-excludability and non-rivalry; free-rider problem; why private markets under-provide public goods; quasi-public goods

nodes.label: Merit & Demerit Goods
nodes.description: Under-consumption of merit goods and over-consumption of demerit goods; imperfect information; paternalistic intervention

nodes.label: Information Failure
nodes.description: Imperfect and asymmetric information; moral hazard; adverse selection; impact on market outcomes

nodes.label: Market Dominance & Abuse of Market Power
nodes.description: Monopoly power leading to market failure; deadweight loss from restricted output; X-inefficiency; barriers to entry

nodes.label: Factor Immobility & Income Inequality
nodes.description: Occupational and geographical immobility of factors; equity vs efficiency; income and wealth inequality as market outcome

nodes.label: Taxes as Government Intervention
nodes.description: Indirect taxes to correct negative externalities; ad valorem vs specific taxes; tax incidence; effectiveness and limitations

nodes.label: Subsidies as Government Intervention
nodes.description: Subsidies to correct positive externalities and encourage merit goods; effectiveness; potential government failure

nodes.label: Regulation & Legislation
nodes.description: Rules and standards; bans; pollution permits; tradable permits; command-and-control vs market-based regulation

nodes.label: Direct Provision & Nationalisation
nodes.description: Government as producer of public goods and essential services; direct provision for merit goods; pros and cons

nodes.label: Price Controls
nodes.description: Price ceilings (maximum prices) and price floors (minimum prices); shortages and surpluses; welfare analysis; examples

nodes.label: Government Failure
nodes.description: When government intervention leads to a net welfare loss; unintended consequences; information gaps; administrative costs; political considerations

nodes.label: Costs of Production
nodes.description: Fixed and variable costs; total, average and marginal costs; short-run and long-run cost curves; economies and diseconomies of scale

nodes.label: Revenue & Profit
nodes.description: Total, average and marginal revenue; normal vs supernormal profit; profit maximisation condition (MC=MR); shut-down decision

nodes.label: Economies & Diseconomies of Scale
nodes.description: Internal economies of scale (technical, managerial, financial, marketing, risk-bearing); external economies; diseconomies of scale; MES

nodes.label: Perfect Competition
nodes.description: Characteristics; price-taking behaviour; short-run and long-run equilibrium; allocative and productive efficiency; diagrams

nodes.label: Monopoly
nodes.description: Sources of monopoly power; barriers to entry; price and output determination; welfare loss; natural monopoly; price discrimination

nodes.label: Monopolistic Competition
nodes.description: Characteristics; product differentiation; short-run and long-run equilibrium; excess capacity; non-price competition

nodes.label: Oligopoly
nodes.description: Characteristics; mutual interdependence; game theory and prisoners' dilemma; kinked demand curve; collusion vs competition; cartels

nodes.label: Price Discrimination
nodes.description: First, second and third degree price discrimination; conditions necessary; welfare effects; real-world examples

nodes.label: Contestable Markets
nodes.description: Theory of contestable markets; sunk costs; hit-and-run entry; implications for efficiency and pricing behaviour

nodes.label: Competition Policy
nodes.description: Anti-trust legislation; regulation of monopoly; merger policy; Singapore's Competition Commission (CCCS); promoting contestability

nodes.label: Measuring National Income
nodes.description: GDP, GNI, NNI; expenditure, income and output approaches; nominal vs real GDP; GDP deflator

nodes.label: Standard of Living
nodes.description: Material vs non-material living standards; GDP per capita as indicator; limitations of GDP; alternative measures (HDI, Gini coefficient)

nodes.label: Circular Flow of Income
nodes.description: Two-sector, three-sector and four-sector circular flow models; injections and withdrawals; equilibrium national income

nodes.label: Aggregate Demand
nodes.description: Components of AD (C + I + G + X - M); determinants of each component; reasons for downward-sloping AD; shifts of AD

nodes.label: Aggregate Supply
nodes.description: Short-run AS vs long-run AS; Keynesian vs Classical AS; factors shifting SRAS and LRAS; productive capacity

nodes.label: Macroeconomic Equilibrium
nodes.description: Determination of equilibrium national income, price level and employment via AD-AS model; inflationary and deflationary gaps

nodes.label: Multiplier Effect
nodes.description: The Keynesian multiplier; MPC, MPS, MPT, MPM; size of the multiplier; limitations and real-world applicability

nodes.label: Causes of Inflation
nodes.description: Demand-pull inflation; cost-push inflation; imported inflation; excessive monetary growth; inflationary expectations

nodes.label: Consequences of Inflation & Deflation
nodes.description: Effects on consumers, firms, government, debtors/creditors; shoe-leather and menu costs; deflation risks; measuring inflation (CPI)

nodes.label: Types of Unemployment
nodes.description: Cyclical (demand-deficient); structural; frictional; seasonal; classical (real wage) unemployment; natural rate of unemployment

nodes.label: Consequences of Unemployment
nodes.description: Individual, social and economic costs; loss of output; fiscal burden; hysteresis; relationship between unemployment and inflation (Phillips curve)

nodes.label: Economic Growth
nodes.description: Actual vs potential growth; short-run vs long-run growth; sources of growth (labour, capital, technology, institutions)

nodes.label: Sustainable & Inclusive Growth
nodes.description: Costs and benefits of growth; environmental sustainability; inclusive growth; income distribution; Singapore's growth strategy

nodes.label: Balance of Payments: Structure
nodes.description: Current account (trade in goods, services, primary income, secondary income); capital and financial account; BOP must balance

nodes.label: Balance of Payments: Issues & Policies
nodes.description: Causes of current account deficits/surpluses; consequences; Marshall-Lerner condition; J-curve effect; corrective policies

nodes.label: Exchange Rates
nodes.description: Determination of exchange rates; demand and supply of currencies; appreciation and depreciation; factors affecting exchange rates

nodes.label: Exchange Rate Systems
nodes.description: Fixed, floating and managed float systems; advantages and disadvantages; Singapore's managed float (BBC) regime under MAS

nodes.label: Fiscal Policy
nodes.description: Government expenditure and taxation; expansionary vs contractionary; budget deficit/surplus; automatic stabilisers; limitations

nodes.label: Monetary Policy
nodes.description: Interest rate targeting; money supply management; transmission mechanism; Singapore's exchange rate-centred monetary policy via MAS

nodes.label: Supply-Side Policies
nodes.description: Market-based and interventionist supply-side policies; education and training; R&D incentives; deregulation; labour market reforms; Singapore context

nodes.label: Policy Conflicts & Trade-offs
nodes.description: Conflicts between macroeconomic objectives; inflation vs unemployment; growth vs environment; short-run vs long-run trade-offs; policy mix

nodes.label: Comparative Advantage & Basis for Trade
nodes.description: Absolute and comparative advantage; gains from specialisation and trade; assumptions and limitations of the theory

nodes.label: Benefits of Free Trade
nodes.description: Consumer benefits (lower prices, greater variety); producer benefits (larger markets, economies of scale); dynamic efficiency gains

nodes.label: Protectionism
nodes.description: Tariffs, quotas, subsidies, embargoes and other trade barriers; arguments for and against protectionism; welfare analysis of tariffs

nodes.label: Terms of Trade
nodes.description: Definition and calculation; causes of changes in terms of trade; impact on the balance of payments and living standards

nodes.label: Economic Integration & Trade Agreements
nodes.description: Free trade areas, customs unions, common markets; WTO; bilateral and regional FTAs; Singapore's FTA network (ASEAN, CPTPP, RCEP)

nodes.label: Globalisation
nodes.description: Drivers of globalisation; trade, capital and labour flows; TNCs/MNCs; benefits and costs of globalisation for developed and developing countries

nodes.label: Globalisation & the Singapore Economy
nodes.description: Singapore as a small open economy; vulnerability to external shocks; trade dependence; FDI strategy; policy responses to globalisation
